top10.introHtml: Refresh 2026-07-12: 18 Cars.com listings (18 with photos, pre-seat filter: 25), 16 CarGurus listings (13 with photos, pre-seat filter: 16; 3 listings could not be stored due to ID extraction limit), and 24 AutoTrader listings (24 with photos, pre-seat filter: 51). TrueCar skipped (body filter broken). Excluded 2-seat roadsters: all Mercedes SL-Class, Ford Thunderbird (retro), Porsche Boxster, Toyota Paseo, MINI Roadster, Nissan 350Z/370Z. Jaguar XJS included as known 4-seat model per task config. Only 2 confirmed manual transmissions found this refresh: 2007 MINI Cooper S and 1997 Camaro Z28 (AutoTrader); additional manuals may exist among listings with null transmission — verify with each dealer/seller.

picks.whyGood: The CLK 350 Cabriolet (W209) is a grand-touring 4-seater with a smooth 3.5L V6, elegant folding soft-top, and proper back seat. At 65k miles it's barely run in for a Mercedes, and 2 miles from Media means easy inspection. Fair Deal pricing from CarGurus suggests it's at market — not a steal, but there's room to negotiate.
picks.whatToVerify: Full top cycle (raise and lower 3×), check for hydraulic top motor noise or slow operation; inspect headliner and rear carpet for water intrusion; check rocker panels for rust; CLK-specific: check for oil leaks at valve cover and rear main seal; verify cooling system condition (CLK V6 can run warm); ask for service history.
picks.bottomLine: Best all-around pick — V6 GT cabriolet, low miles, literally in your backyard.

picks.whyGood: The Cascada is America's last modern 4-seat GM convertible — roomy, comfortable, and far more refined than a Sebring. The 1.6T turbo four is peppy enough for weekend fun, the soft-top stows quickly, and Great Deal pricing means you're buying at or below market. 71k miles is low for a 2016.
picks.whatToVerify: Full top cycle including rear window; check top seals along the A-pillar and deck lid for leaks; inspect the drain channels behind the rear seat (Cascada-specific weak point); verify turbo boost response; check for rust around trunk seal.
picks.bottomLine: Most modern, most practical 4-seat convertible in the list — Great Deal with 71k miles.

picks.whyGood: The S197 Mustang (2010–2014 refresh) convertible is the sweet spot for cheap fun. Parts are cheap, mechanics know them, and the V6 makes 305hp. 9 miles from Media means you can easily PPI it at a local shop. 96k miles is nothing for a Mustang engine.
picks.whatToVerify: Top cycle and rear-window seam; check for water staining on rear seat backs and trunk carpet; inspect convertible top latch mechanism; Mustang-specific: check 8.8 axle for clunk on takeoff; verify power steering fluid level; look for evidence of track or autocross use.
picks.bottomLine: Closest Mustang to Media, well-priced for a 2012 — solid weekend fun.

picks.whyGood: The A5 Cabriolet is Audi's best-looking convertible ever — proper 4 seats, a beautifully-sewn soft-top, and the 2.0T engine that runs forever if you keep up on oil changes. Only 57k miles at under $10k is exceptional value. Clean title and no accidents on Cars.com.
picks.whatToVerify: Top hydraulic pump noise (slow top = early pump failure, ~$600); check rear window delamination on the plastic rear window; inspect for oil leaks from the 2.0T timing chain tensioner area; verify DSG fluid service history; check cabin air filter and sunroof drain channels.
picks.bottomLine: Best value A5 Cabriolet in the region — 57k miles under $10k is a steal if it's clean.

picks.whyGood: The F57 MINI Convertible (2016-gen platform at 2015 pricing) with 55k miles from a private seller 8 miles away is hard to beat for proximity. MINIs hold up well when maintained, the back seat fits kids or luggage, and the folding soft-top drops in 18 seconds at up to 18 mph.
picks.whatToVerify: Verify transmission type (could be 6-speed manual or automatic — confirm before offer); top fabric condition and operation; check cooling system (MINI water pumps and thermostats are known weak points); verify oil service history; check for rust around rear wheel arches.
picks.bottomLine: Private seller, 8 miles away, 55k miles — easiest to inspect and most likely negotiable.

picks.whyGood: The B7 A4 Cabriolet quattro (2007) with 62k miles and a Great Deal flag is a legitimate find. All-wheel drive is a bonus for year-round light use. The 2.0T is solid at this mileage if the timing belt/chain has been serviced. 23 miles from Media, dealer with reviews.
picks.whatToVerify: Confirm transmission (manual or auto — ask dealer); verify timing chain tensioner service; check top hydraulic operation (top pump) and all 4 quarter-window seals; inspect for rust on rear subframe; ask for inspection history on the multitronic CVT if auto.
picks.bottomLine: AWD Cabriolet at 62k flagged Great Deal — worthy PPI candidate in Willow Grove.

picks.whyGood: The only confirmed manual convertible under $9k in the dataset. The R52 MINI Cooper S with 6-speed manual is a pure-driving machine — 172hp supercharged engine, go-kart handling, proper stick shift. 65k miles is low for this generation. Best manual buy in the region.
picks.whatToVerify: Clutch engagement point (high = worn disc, budget $800 replacement); 2nd–3rd gear synchro slow shift test; supercharger belt and coupler condition; check for water in the spare tire well (common leak point); inspect top fabric and rear window seam; check coolant level (R52 water pumps can fail).
picks.bottomLine: Only confirmed manual convertible under $10k — the driver's pick of this entire list.

picks.whyGood: The F33 428i Convertible is the most modern BMW in the list. The N20 2.0T produces 240hp, the electric soft-top drops in 20 seconds, and rear seats are genuinely usable. At 103k miles and Good Deal pricing, this is strong value if it has service history.
picks.whatToVerify: N20 engine: timing chain stretch at high mileage (rumble on cold start = red flag); check for oil leaks at valve cover and oil filter housing; verify 8-speed ZF trans fluid service; full top cycle; check for water intrusion at rear corners of convertible top; test heated seat elements.
picks.bottomLine: Most current BMW convertible in budget — Good Deal at 19 miles if the timing chain is healthy.

picks.whyGood: The C70 retractable hardtop is the quietest, most weather-tight convertible in this list. No fabric to rot, no rear-window to cloud. The T5 turbocharged 5-cylinder is strong and smooth. 93k miles is reasonable for a 2011. Clean title on Cars.com.
picks.whatToVerify: Full hardtop cycle (3 complete open/close): listen for motor strain or panel misalignment; inspect the seals around the folding roof panels for cracking; verify Geartronic transmission fluid has been serviced; check for coolant leaks from the turbo coolant line; inspect roof lining for headliner sag.
picks.bottomLine: Best top engineering in the group — the retractable hardtop is simply more durable than fabric.

picks.whyGood: Cheapest S197 Mustang in the dataset. The 2006 Mustang GT 4.6L V8 (or the 4.0L V6 at this price) is a proven platform with cheap parts everywhere. 118k miles on a Mustang is nothing — these engines run to 200k with regular oil changes. At $7,598 there's room for a PPI and still come in under $8k.
picks.whatToVerify: Top latch mechanism and rear window seal (early S197 tops are prone to latch wear); water staining on rear seat and trunk carpet; check 4.6 cam phasers if GT (rattles on cold start 2005–2010); 8.8 rear axle clunk test; rust check on rear quarters and trunk floor; verify power-top motor operation.
picks.bottomLine: Cheapest path to a Mustang convertible — rough miles but rock-solid mechanicals if it's a V8.

manual10.introHtml: Manual convertibles are rare in this price band and 35-mile radius. Only 2 confirmed manual listings surfaced this refresh: a 2007 MINI Cooper S ($8,452, 65k mi) and a 1997 Camaro Z28 ($11,895, 73k mi), both on AutoTrader. Multiple BMW, MINI, and Audi listings show null transmission — some may be manual. Always confirm with the seller before making a trip.

picks.whyGood: R52 MINI Cooper S with 6-speed manual and supercharged engine — the most fun-per-dollar manual in this search. 65k miles means it's barely past the break-in point for a MINI. Easy to find a MINI-literate indie shop in the Media/Chester County area.
picks.whatToVerify: Clutch bite point (high = worn, budget $800); 2nd–3rd synchro shift quality; supercharger belt and coupler condition (common wear item); check for water in spare tire well; inspect top seam and rear window.
picks.bottomLine: Sole manual convertible under $9k in the dataset — the obvious #1 pick for a driver.

picks.whyGood: 4th-gen Camaro Z28 convertible with T56 6-speed manual and the LT1 V8 — a legitimate muscle car for $12k. 73k miles is low for a 1997. These cars have a devoted parts/community ecosystem, and the 4-seat rear is functional for smaller adults.
picks.whatToVerify: Clutch engagement point and pedal feel; T56 2nd/3rd gear synchro test (slow into 2nd = worn synchro); throw-out bearing chirp at idle; check convertible top motor (roof motor failures are common on 4th-gen Camaros); inspect for rust on the convertible top well and rear trunk areas; verify cooling system (LT1 opti-spark concerns were fixed by ~1996 but verify).
picks.bottomLine: Only V8 manual convertible in the dataset — raw, American, and genuinely fun.

mustangs.introHtml: Three 2005+ Mustang convertibles found this refresh. All are automatic. The 2018 is the newest (S550 platform, IRS, best handling) but has 141k miles. The 2012 is the best value-to-distance ratio at 9 miles from Media. The 2006 is the cheapest entry point at $7,598.

picks.whyGood: The newest Mustang convertible in the dataset — S550 platform with a much better independent rear suspension than the S197. The 3.7L V6 or 2.3L EcoBoost at this trim level makes a strong freeway-entry ramp car. Premium trim means Sync 3, heated seats, and the 9-speaker audio. High miles but S550 Mustangs are built tough.
picks.whatToVerify: MT-82 6-speed (if manual): shifter notchiness is known; verify rear camera works; check top seal at A-pillars and rear corners; S550 EcoBoost: verify Cobb tune or aftermarket tune hasn't been added; check for water stains on rear seat from top-seal failure; 141k miles — verify transmission fluid service.
picks.bottomLine: Newest, best-handling Mustang in the search — high miles but S550 platform is worth it.

picks.whyGood: The 2012 S197 refresh hit the sweet spot: updated interior, 305hp V6, decent fuel economy, and solid build. Only 9 miles from Media in West Chester means a trivial pre-purchase inspection. 96k miles is light duty for a Mustang drivetrain.
picks.whatToVerify: Top latch and rear window seal integrity; water staining on rear seat and carpet; 4.6 V8 cam phasers if GT (cold-start rattle); check rear axle for clunk on takeoff; inspect convertible top hydraulic lines for seeps; verify power steering operation.
picks.bottomLine: Closest 2005+ Mustang to Media — 9 miles away makes the PPI trivial.

picks.whyGood: Cheapest 2005+ Mustang in the dataset by $3,800. The S197 first-gen (2005–2009) looks great and the 4.0L V6 or 4.6L V8 is proven to 200k+ miles. At $7,598 you have budget left for a PPI and any needed maintenance. Worth driving to Westampton NJ if it's clean.
picks.whatToVerify: S197-specific: spring perch rust check (common on early cars); top latch wear; cam phaser rattle on 3-valve 4.6 GT (2005–2010); rear axle clunk; water intrusion at rear seat; check if drivetrain is V6 or V8 before making the trip.
picks.bottomLine: Best-value 2005+ Mustang in the radius — $7,598 leaves money for a thorough PPI.
